zh.Echingham, Sussex, England: 英國英格蘭素可艾欽加姆
zh.Dartford, Kent, England: 英國英格蘭肯特達特福德
zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Criol於1336年出生在英國肯特時，她的父親Sir John de Criol31歲，母親Lettice26歲。她於大約1352年與Sir William Echyngham在英國素可結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1391年之後在英國素可艾欽加姆。

zh-hans.Echingham, Sussex, England: 英国英格兰素可艾欽加姆
zh-hans.Dartford, Kent, England: 英国英格兰肯特達特福德
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Criol于1336年出生在英国肯特时，她的父亲Sir John de Criol31岁，母亲Lettice26岁。她于大约1352年与Sir William Echyngham在英国素可结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1391年之后在英国素可艾欽加姆。

nameMeanings.name: Criol
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Alberto, Hipolito, Luis, Eriberto, Jose, Luisa, Ramon, Ricardo.  French/Haitian Edwige, Fritz, Serge, Yvrose.
Catalan: from the old personal name Oriol, from Latin Aureolus.

Catalan and French: nickname, possibly for a flamboyant dresser or sweet singer, from Catalan and Old French oriol ‘oriole’ (from Latin aureolus ‘golden’), a species of bird of which the male has bright yellow and black plumage. As a surname of French origin it is also common in Haiti.

variants.name: Oriel
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English and Old French female personal name Oriold, Oriel, from ancient Germanic Aurildis, Orieldis, composed of elements meaning ‘fire’ + ‘strife’.
French (Vosges): nickname from Old French oriel, a variant of oriol ‘oriole’ (see Oriol ).

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Criol was born in 1336, in Kent, England, her father, Sir John de Criol, was 31 and her mother, Lettice, was 26. She married Sir William Echyngham about 1352, in Sussex, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died after 1391, in Etchingham, Sussex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Criol est née en 1336, à Kent, Angleterre, son père, Sir John de Criol, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Lettice, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William Echyngham environ 1352, à Sussex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée après 1391, à Etchingham, Sussex, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Criol 1336 in Kent, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John de Criol 31 und ihre Mutter Lettice 26. Sie hat um 1352 in Sussex, England Sir William Echyngham geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1391 in Etchingham, Sussex, England.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Criol è nata il nel 1336 nel Kent, England, sua padre, Sir John de Criol, aveva 31 anni e sua madre, Lettice, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Sir William Echyngham circa 1352, nel Sussex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì dopo il 1391, nel Etchingham, Sussex, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Criolが1336年にイギリスケントで生まれた時，父Sir John de Criolは31歳，母Letticeは26歳であった。約1352年にイギリススセックスでSir William Echynghamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1391年以後にイギリススセックスエトチンガムで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Criol(이)가 1336년 영국 켄트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John de Criol(은)는 31세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Lettice(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 대략 1352년 영국 수세스에서 Sir William Echyngham(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1391년 이후 영국 수세스 트친그함에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Criol nasceu em 1336, em Kent, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John de Criol, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Lettice, tinha 26 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir William Echyngham aproximadamente 1352, em Sussex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1391, em Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Criol родилась в 1336 г. в местности кэнт, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John de Criol было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Lettice, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William Echyngham около 1352 г. в местности суссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла после 1391 г. в местности эчингхам, суссекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Criol nació en 1336, en Kent, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John de Criol, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Lettice, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir William Echyngham aproximadamente 1352, en Sussex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió después de 1391, en Etchingham, Sussex, Inglaterra.
